questions.question: For `sleep`, an argument without a unit suffix defaults to seconds.
questions.explanation: Correct. For sleep, each argument defaults to seconds if no unit suffix is given.
questions.source_section: 25.1 sleep: Delay for a specified time
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The `timeout --kill-after` option ensures the monitored command is also sent a KILL signal.
questions.explanation: Correct. timeout --kill-after option ensures the monitored command is killed by also sending a KILL signal.
questions.source_section: 23.6 timeout: Run a command with a time limit
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: A `nohup.out` file created by `nohup` follows the current umask without any exception.
questions.explanation: Incorrect. Any nohup.out or $HOME/nohup.out file created by nohup is made readable and writable only to the user, regardless of current umask settings.
questions.source_section: 23.4 nohup: Run a command immune to hangups
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: If `nice --adjustment` is negative and you lack appropriate privileges, `nice` warns and otherwise behaves as if the adjustment were zero.
questions.explanation: Correct. If the nice --adjustment value is negative and you lack appropriate privileges, nice issues a warning and otherwise acts as if you specified a zero adjustment.
questions.source_section: 23.3 nice: Run a command with modified niceness
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The command managed by `timeout` may be a shell special built-in utility.
questions.explanation: Incorrect. command run by timeout must not be a special built-in utility.
questions.source_section: 23.6 timeout: Run a command with a time limit
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: To change the niceness of an existing process, use `renice` rather than `nice`.
questions.explanation: Correct. To change the niceness of an existing process, use the renice command.
questions.source_section: 23.3 nice: Run a command with modified niceness
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: In `timeout`, a duration of 0 forces the associated timeout to expire immediately.
questions.explanation: Incorrect. A timeout duration of 0 disables the associated timeout.
questions.source_section: 23.6 timeout: Run a command with a time limit
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: If standard error is a terminal, `nohup` normally redirects it to the same file descriptor as standard output.
questions.explanation: Correct. If standard error is a terminal, nohup normally redirects it to the same file descriptor as standard output.
questions.source_section: 23.4 nohup: Run a command immune to hangups
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The `nohup` command automatically lowers the niceness of the command it runs so background work is less intrusive.
questions.explanation: Incorrect. nohup command does not alter the niceness of the command it runs.
questions.source_section: 23.4 nohup: Run a command immune to hangups
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: Niceness is exactly the same thing as scheduling priority and must be obeyed by the scheduler.
questions.explanation: Incorrect. Niceness should not be confused with scheduling priority because niceness is merely advice to the scheduler and may be ignored.
questions.source_section: 23.3 nice: Run a command with modified niceness
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: For `timeout`, an unsuffixed duration is measured in minutes by default.
questions.explanation: Incorrect. For timeout, the default duration unit is seconds.
questions.source_section: 23.6 timeout: Run a command with a time limit
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The `timeout --kill-after` option has no effect if the main timeout duration or the kill-after duration is 0.
questions.explanation: Correct. timeout --kill-after option has no effect if the main timeout duration or the kill-after duration is 0.
questions.source_section: 23.6 timeout: Run a command with a time limit
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The `timeout` command runs a command and kills it if it is still running after the specified time interval.
questions.explanation: Correct. timeout command runs the given command and kills it if it is still running after the specified time interval.
questions.source_section: 23.6 timeout: Run a command with a time limit
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: By default, running a command through `nice` decreases its niceness by 10.
questions.explanation: Incorrect. When running a command, nice increments its niceness by 10 by default.
questions.source_section: 23.3 nice: Run a command with modified niceness
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The `nohup` command ignores hangup signals so a command can continue running after logout.
questions.explanation: Correct. nohup command runs the given command with hangup signals ignored so that it can continue running after logout.
questions.source_section: 23.4 nohup: Run a command immune to hangups
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The `timeout --signal` option can send a chosen signal instead of the default TERM signal.
questions.explanation: Correct. timeout --signal option sends the specified signal on timeout instead of the default TERM signal.
questions.source_section: 23.6 timeout: Run a command with a time limit
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: If standard input is a terminal, `nohup` leaves it unchanged so the command can keep reading from the terminal normally.
questions.explanation: Incorrect. If standard input is a terminal, nohup redirects it and makes the substitute file descriptor unreadable.
questions.source_section: 23.4 nohup: Run a command immune to hangups
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: For `timeout --kill-after`, the extra duration is measured from when the command starts, not from when the first timeout signal is sent.
questions.explanation: Incorrect. duration for timeout --kill-after starts when timeout sends the initial signal, not when the command is started.
questions.source_section: 23.6 timeout: Run a command with a time limit
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The `nohup` command automatically backgrounds the command it runs.
questions.explanation: Incorrect. nohup command does not automatically put the command it runs in the background.
questions.source_section: 23.4 nohup: Run a command immune to hangups
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: Without `--preserve-status`, `timeout` uses exit status 124 when the managed command times out.
questions.explanation: Correct. timeout command exits with status 124 if the managed command times out and --preserve-status is not specified.
questions.source_section: 23.6 timeout: Run a command with a time limit
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: With `timeout --preserve-status`, a timeout still always returns a fixed timeout status instead of the managed command's status.
questions.explanation: Incorrect. timeout --preserve-status option returns the exit status of the managed command on timeout rather than a specific timeout status.
questions.source_section: 23.6 timeout: Run a command with a time limit
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The `timeout --foreground` option avoids creating a separate background program group so the command can use the foreground TTY normally.
questions.explanation: Correct. timeout --foreground option does not create a separate background program group, so the managed command can use the foreground TTY normally.
questions.source_section: 23.6 timeout: Run a command with a time limit
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: If standard output is a terminal, `nohup` tries `nohup.out`, then `$HOME/nohup.out`, and does not run the command if neither can be written.
questions.explanation: Correct. If standard output is a terminal, nohup appends standard output to nohup.out, falls back to $HOME/nohup.out if needed, and does not run the command if neither file can be written.
questions.source_section: 23.4 nohup: Run a command immune to hangups
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The `timeout` command accepts duration suffixes for seconds, minutes, hours, and days.
questions.explanation: Correct. timeout command accepts duration suffixes for seconds, minutes, hours, and days.
questions.source_section: 23.6 timeout: Run a command with a time limit
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The `nproc` command always prints the total number of online processors, never a smaller process-limited value.
questions.explanation: Incorrect. nproc command shows the number of processing units available to the current process, which may be less than the number of online processors.
questions.source_section: 21.3 nproc: Print the number of available processors
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: The `sleep` command pauses for only the first command-line duration argument and ignores any remaining ones.
questions.explanation: Incorrect. sleep command pauses for an amount of time specified by the sum of the values of the command line arguments.
questions.source_section: 25.1 sleep: Delay for a specified time
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: A niceness of -20 represents higher scheduling favor than a niceness of 19.
questions.explanation: Correct. A niceness of -20 is high priority, while 19 is lower priority and has less impact on other running processes.
questions.source_section: 23.3 nice: Run a command with modified niceness
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: If no arguments are given, `nice` prints the current niceness.
questions.explanation: Correct. If no arguments are given, the nice command prints the current niceness.
questions.source_section: 23.3 nice: Run a command with modified niceness
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: In `timeout --foreground` mode, all children of the managed command are still guaranteed to be timed out.
questions.explanation: Incorrect. In timeout foreground mode, any children of the managed command will not be timed out.
questions.source_section: 23.6 timeout: Run a command with a time limit
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.

questions.question: If a KILL(9) signal is involved, `timeout` returns exit status 124.
questions.explanation: Incorrect. timeout command exits with status 137 if the managed command or timeout itself is sent the KILL(9) signal.
questions.source_section: 23.6 timeout: Run a command with a time limit
questions.reason: Approved in final review after source and source_ir_id confirmation.
